3  Trips+ E-Services Modules
Taxpayers accessing Trips+ E-Services are supported in the following ways;  Introduction
Trips+ E-Services provides Revenue authorities with the tools required to support the configuration of their 
business process workflows as well as their business rules. 
Trips+ E-Services Improves the interaction between Taxpayers and Tax Authorities by delivering a robust and 
Easy to use Platform allowing all stakeholders to leverage the following advantages: 
 
• Faster Processing: 
         Submissions are online and promptly acted on thus faster turnaround time
• Improved Accuracy
          In Build validation rules will identify errors immediately and prompt the tax- payers to make corrections     
          thus minimizing entry errors 
• Convenience
         Trips+ E-services is deployed with high availability in mind thus is available 23/7 and users can access it at    
         any time and from anywhere 
• Instant Feedback 
         Trips+ E-Services allows for Prompt feedback to the Taxpayers upon submission of all transactions.
• Providing information and 
assistance to the Taxpayer 
through multiple channels such 
as mobile, web, video and voice.
Trips+ E-Services is integrated with the Tax Authorities ITAS. To the users, the integration is seamless. They 
simply access the portal and select the menu options required for the actions they wish to achieve.  
Taxpayer documents submitted on-line are seamlessly transmitted from the portal to the core services.• Enabling Taxpayers to transact 
business with the tax authority 
24/7 from anywhere in the 
world.01 | E-Registration 02 | E-Filing 03 | E-PaymentsTrips+ E-Services Comprises of the following three modules

Trips+ E-Registration provides a centralized Taxpayer 
Registry of the highest integrity. The registration 
process is simple, immediate and user friendly, with 
the Taxpayer receiving immediate validation and 
receipt of the submission.  
Trips+ E-Registration enables the full registration of 
individuals, corporations and other legal entities.When the initial registration application is approved, 
Trips+ E-Registration generates a unique TIN, 
advises the Taxpayer through automated notices 
and provides the necessary certification Additional 
information is obtained during subsequent Taxpayer 
registration interactions. Trips+ E-Registration1

5Return Filing
E – Filling FeaturesTrips+ E-Filing fully supports the Taxpayer in the returns process and will be configured to automatically 
send auto-alerts to taxpayers on a calendar or event driven basis and provide systematic assistance and 
guidance to Taxpayers.
01 02 03 04 05
Before the return 
is submitted, it 
is automatically 
assessed and 
analyzed for accuracy 
and duplications. Returns are never 
physically deleted 
from the system. If 
an error occurs, the 
return is ‘saved’ and 
the system allows 
the taxpayer to make 
the necessary cor-
rections before sub-
mitting the return for 
processing. A uniquely numbered 
receipt is generated 
and sent to the 
Taxpayer when the 
return is successfully 
submitted and posted 
in the system. Authorized officers 
can initiate an 
amended return 
business process and 
authorized users can 
view returns online. For paper returns 
requiring manual 
entry after 
lodgment and 
receipt processing, 
the system creates 
tasks to ensure that 
additional details are 
processed on a timely 
basis.
• Trips+ supports electronic 
payments through the Portal, 
Other Payment Gateways and 
Banks. • Taxpayers can pay their liabilities 
using several different methods 
such as mobile, bank, inter-bank 
transfer and debit/credit card 
payments. • Payments are interfaced to the 
Tax Authorities ITAS, posted to 
a Taxpayer’s ledgers, and used 
to offset outstanding tax-type 
liabilities in accordance with tax 
administration’s business rules. • Trips+ E-Filing Module Supports 
electronic submissions online 
return forms.• The Taxpayer is assisted to 
accurately complete returns by 
aids.• Automated calculation of the 
Taxpayer’s liability or refund. 
• The E-Filing module also supports 
the principle of provisional ‘Self-
Assessed’ returns for income tax. • A provisional return is expected 
for registered taxpayers with 
estimates of Chargeable income 
for the next financial year. • The system supports various 
combinations of periodic as well 
as nonperiodic return periods for 
tax/revenue streams.
